sections.title: Hedge Funds
sections.content: Hedge funds are pooled investment vehicles that employ various strategies to earn positive returns. They typically have more flexibility than traditional funds.

practiceQuestions.question: A typical hedge fund fee structure is:
practiceQuestions.explanation: The traditional hedge fund fee structure is '2 and 20': 2% management fee on assets and 20% of profits as performance fee.

practiceQuestions.question: A long/short equity hedge fund strategy involves:
options: Buying undervalued stocks and shorting overvalued stocks
practiceQuestions.explanation: Long/short equity funds take long positions in stocks expected to increase and short positions in stocks expected to decrease.

sections.title: Private Equity
sections.content: Private equity involves investing in private companies or taking public companies private. Strategies include venture capital, buyouts, and growth capital.
keyPoints: Illiquidity premium compensates for long lock-ups

practiceQuestions.question: Venture capital primarily invests in:
practiceQuestions.explanation: Venture capital focuses on early-stage, high-growth potential private companies, typically in technology or innovative sectors.

practiceQuestions.question: A leveraged buyout (LBO) is characterized by:
options: High debt financing to acquire a company
practiceQuestions.explanation: An LBO uses significant debt (leverage) to finance the acquisition of a company, with the target's cash flows used to service the debt.

sections.title: Real Estate and Commodities
sections.content: Real estate and commodities provide diversification benefits and inflation hedging. They have unique valuation and risk characteristics.
keyPoints: Real estate provides income and appreciation
REITs offer liquid real estate exposure

formulas.expression: Cap Rate = Net Operating Income / Property Value

practiceQuestions.question: A property with NOI of $100,000 valued at $1,250,000 has a cap rate of:
